title: Compensation for loss or damage caused by breach of contract
description: When a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused to him thereby which naturally arose in the usual course of things from such breach or which the parties knew when they made the contract to be likely to result from the breach of it.

title: Compensation for breach of contract where penalty stipulated
description: When a contract has been broken, if a sum is named in the contract as the amount to be paid in case of such breach, or if the contract contains any other stipulation by way of penalty, the party complaining of the breach is entitled to receive from the party who has broken the contract reasonable compensation not exceeding the amount so named or penalty stipulated for.

title: Effect of failure to perform at fixed time in contract where time is essential
description: When a party to a contract promises to do a certain thing at or before a specified time and fails to do any such thing at or before the specified time, the contract becomes voidable at the option of the promisee if the intention of the parties was that time should be of the essence.

title: Specific performance of contracts
description: The specific performance of a contract shall be enforced by the court when the act agreed to be done is in the performance of a trust, when there exists no standard for ascertaining actual damage caused by non-performance, or when the act agreed to be done is such that monetary compensation for non-performance would not afford adequate relief.

title: Contracts not specifically enforceable
description: The following contracts cannot be specifically enforced: a contract for the non-performance of which compensation in money is an adequate relief; a contract which runs into such minute or numerous details or which is so dependent on the personal qualifications or volition of the parties.

title: Power to refer parties to arbitration where there is an arbitration agreement
description: A judicial authority before which an action is brought in a matter which is the subject of an arbitration agreement shall refer the parties to arbitration unless it finds that prima facie no valid arbitration agreement exists.

title: Appointment of arbitrators
description: A person of any nationality may be an arbitrator unless otherwise agreed by the parties. The appointment of arbitrator shall be made by the Supreme Court or High Court or any person or institution designated by such Court.

title: Application for setting aside arbitral award
description: Recourse to a court against an arbitral award may be made only by an application for setting aside such award. An award may be set aside if the party was under some incapacity, the arbitration agreement is not valid, or the award is in conflict with the public policy of India.

title: Conditions for enforcement of foreign awards
description: Enforcement of a foreign award may be refused if the party against whom it is invoked furnishes proof that the parties were under some incapacity, the agreement is not valid, proper notice was not given, or the award deals with matters not within the submission to arbitration.

title: Summary Suits
description: Courts of competent jurisdiction may try suits upon bills of exchange, hundis, promissory notes, and suits in which the plaintiff seeks to recover a debt or liquidated demand in money payable by the defendant with or without interest arising on a written contract.

title: Temporary Injunctions
description: Where in any suit it is proved by affidavit or otherwise that the property in dispute is in danger of being wasted, damaged, or alienated by any party to the suit, the court may by order grant a temporary injunction to restrain such act.

title: Definitions — Commercial Dispute
description: Commercial dispute means a dispute arising out of ordinary transactions of merchants, bankers, financiers and traders such as disputes relating to mercantile documents, export or import of merchandise, admiralty and maritime law, transactions relating to aircraft, real estate used for commercial purposes.

title: Determination of Specified Value
description: The specified value of the subject matter of a commercial dispute shall be determined in the manner provided under this section. Where the relief sought is for recovery of money, the money sought to be recovered shall be taken into account for determining specified value.

title: Moratorium
description: On insolvency commencement date, the Adjudicating Authority shall by order declare moratorium prohibiting institution of suits or continuation of pending suits or proceedings against the corporate debtor including execution of any judgment.

title: Application for initiation of corporate insolvency resolution process by operational creditor
description: After expiry of the period of ten days from the date of delivery of the notice or invoice demanding payment, if the operational creditor does not receive payment or notice of dispute, the operational creditor may file an application before the Adjudicating Authority for initiating corporate insolvency resolution process.

title: Temporary Injunctions
description: Order 39 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 deals with the grant of temporary or interim injunctions by the court. It empowers the court to issue orders to maintain the status quo and prevent irreparable harm, such as restraining a party from transferring property, invoking a bank guarantee, or taking actions that may affect the subject matter of the dispute until the final decision is made.

title: Power to refer parties to arbitration
description: Section 8 of the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996 deals with the stay of proceedings in respect of an arbitral proceeding. It provides that where a party to an arbitral proceeding applies for a stay of proceedings, the court may, if it is satisfied that the application is made in good faith and that there is a prima facie case, grant such stay.
